title: rhus radicans var. divaricata
intro: Rhus radicans var. divaricata, also known as poison ivy, is a highly allergenic plant native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral compounds. The plant's extracts have been used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations, due to its health benefits like reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of Rhus radicans var. divaricata, such as tinctures and salves, can be used topically to soothe skin irritations and internally to treat fever and other health issues, although it is essential to use caution and proper preparation to avoid its allergenic properties.
